task.en: Create a shell script at `~/Documents/today.sh` that prints today's date in `YYYY-MM-DD` format and nothing else. Running `bash ~/Documents/today.sh` should output exactly one line matching `YYYY-MM-DD` (e.g. `2026-05-27`). You can use TextEdit (Plain Text mode) or any editor to create the file.

grading_command: OUT=$(bash "$HOME/Documents/today.sh" 2>/dev/null); echo "$OUT" | grep -qE '^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}$' && echo True || echo False
